2603.18390.title: AutoScreen-FW: An LLM-based Framework for Resume Screening
2603.18390.paper_summary: Corporate recruiters often need to screen many resumes within a limited time, which increases their burden and may cause suitable candidates to be overlooked. To address these challenges, prior work has explored LLM-based automated resume screening. However, some methods rely on commercial LLMs, which may pose data privacy risks. Moreover, since companies typically do not make resumes with evaluation results publicly available, it remains unclear which resume samples should be used during learning to improve an LLM's judgment performance. To address these problems, we propose AutoScreen-FW, an LLM-based locally and automatically resume screening framework. AutoScreen-FW uses several methods to select a small set of representative resume samples. These samples are used for in-context learning together with a persona description and evaluation criteria, enabling open-source LLMs to act as a career advisor and evaluate unseen resumes. Experiments with multiple ground truths show that the open-source LLM judges consistently outperform GPT-5-nano. Under one ground truth setting, it also surpass GPT-5-mini. Although it is slightly weaker than GPT-5-mini under other ground-truth settings, it runs substantially faster per resume than commercial GPT models. These findings indicate the potential for deploying AutoScreen-FW locally in companies to support efficient screening while reducing recruiters' burden.
2603.18390.paper_summary_zh: 企業招聘人員經常需要在有限的時間內篩選大量簡歷，這增加了他們的負擔，並可能導致合適的候選人被忽視。為了解決這些挑戰，先前的研究探索了基於LLM的自動簡歷篩選方法。然而，一些方法依賴於商業LLM，這可能會帶來數據隱私風險。此外，由於公司通常不會將帶有評估結果的簡歷公開，因此在學習過程中應使用哪些簡歷樣本以提高LLM的判斷性能仍然不清楚。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了AutoScreen-FW，一個基於LLM的本地自動簡歷篩選框架。AutoScreen-FW使用幾種方法來選擇一小組代表性的簡歷樣本。這些樣本與角色描述和評估標準一起用於上下文學習，使開源LLM能夠充當職業顧問並評估未見的簡歷。多個真實情況下的實驗顯示，開源LLM的評判表現始終優於GPT-5-nano。在一個真實情況設置下，它還超越了GPT-5-mini。儘管在其他真實情況設置下，它的表現略遜於GPT-5-mini，但每份簡歷的運行速度比商業GPT模型快得多。這些發現表明，在公司內部本地部署AutoScreen-FW以支持高效篩選同時減輕招聘人員負擔的潛力。
2603.18390.author: Zhelin Xu et.al.
2603.18390.authors: Zhelin Xu, Shuhei Yamamoto, Atsuyuki Morishima
